evals.prompt: I run a B2B SaaS for HR teams at $299/month. My trial-to-paid conversion is 8% and I can't figure out why people sign up but don't convert. What psychological principles should I apply to my upgrade email sequence and in-app prompts?
evals.expected_output: Analysis using relevant psychological principles (e.g., endowment effect, commitment & consistency, loss aversion, goal-gradient), with specific actionable recommendations for the upgrade email and in-app prompts.
assertions: Identifies at least 3 relevant psychological principles by name
Connects each principle to a specific tactic for the upgrade email or in-app prompt
Addresses the trial experience and what happens in the first session
Mentions loss aversion or framing of what users lose by not upgrading
Provides specific copy suggestions or structural recommendations

evals.prompt: We're launching a $197 online course on LinkedIn content. We want to use scarcity and urgency but I've heard this can backfire. When does scarcity work and when does it feel manipulative?
evals.expected_output: Nuanced analysis of scarcity psychology, distinguishing genuine scarcity from artificial urgency, with specific guidance on ethical application and examples of what works vs. what damages trust.
assertions: Distinguishes between genuine and artificial scarcity
Explains the psychological mechanism behind scarcity (e.g., reactance, loss aversion)
Gives specific examples of scarcity tactics that work vs. those that feel fake
Addresses trust implications and long-term brand effects
Provides a recommendation on whether/how to use scarcity for this specific product

evals.prompt: Help me write a landing page for my project management tool for remote teams
evals.expected_output: Acknowledges this is primarily a copywriting/page-cro task, offers to apply psychological principles to the copy strategy (e.g., anchoring, social proof structure, loss aversion in CTAs), and redirects to copywriting skill for the actual writing.
assertions: Recognizes this is primarily a copywriting task
Offers psychological lens to apply to the copy strategy rather than writing the page itself
Mentions relevant principles (social proof, anchoring, loss aversion) as applicable to landing pages
References or redirects to the copywriting skill for the actual page writing
